maintainer_org: FoundationAgents (MetaGPT team — core authors Xinbin Liang @mannaandpoem, Jinyu Xiang @XiangJinyu, Zhaoyang Yu, Jiayi Zhang, Sirui Hong; all from MetaGPT)
last_commit_at: 2025-04-10 (v0.3.0 release; ~526 commits, 3 releases, 353 open issues, 199 PRs at capture)
execution_kernel_pattern: Layered ReAct -> ToolCall -> Manus inheritance. app/agent/ contains base.py, react.py (ReActAgent base loop), toolcall.py (ToolCallAgent — OpenAI-style function-calling on top of ReAct), and manus.py (the general agent composing tools). A separate planning flow exists via run_flow.py (planner-executor, marked 'unstable multi-agent version'). Effectively a code-act + ReAct hybrid: model emits tool calls, executes, observes, loops — with optional outer planner.

multi_agent_support.notes: Not a full multi-agent debate framework like MetaGPT. Provides specialized agents (Manus general, DataAnalysis, SWE, Browser) selectable via config.toml. run_flow.py composes them under a Planning flow. Multi-agent path explicitly tagged 'unstable' in README.

tool_protocol: Native OpenAI function-calling (ToolCallAgent) + MCP support via run_mcp.py / run_mcp_server.py. Tools include browser automation (Playwright), terminal/code execution (sandbox), file operations, web search/scraping (Crawl4AI), Python execution, and computer-vision-driven GUI interaction.
memory_model: Short-term conversational memory (message history in ReAct loop). No first-class long-term memory store advertised; relies on conversation context window + tool-returned observations.
supported_model_providers: Primary: OpenAI (GPT-4o default). Extensible via base_url override in config.toml, enabling Azure OpenAI, Anthropic-via-proxy, DeepSeek, Ollama, vLLM, and other OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Vision models supported for GUI/browser screenshots.
observability_story: Configuration-driven structured logging via config.toml; no first-class trace UI. Sandbox isolation for tool execution. No native Langfuse/OpenTelemetry integration in core repo at capture.
typical_use_case: General computer-use automation: open-ended tasks that span browser + terminal + filesystem (e.g., 'research X and write a report', data analysis with code execution, browsing + scraping + summarization). Positioned as an open-source clone of the Manus.im product.
maturity_signal: Viral early-2025 launch (56k+ stars within weeks of Manus product controversy), but velocity slowed: last release v0.3.0 in April 2025, with 353 open issues and 199 open PRs suggesting maintainer bandwidth lagging community demand. Active but not yet enterprise-hardened; explicit 'unstable' labels on multi-agent flow. OpenManus-RL spinoff (GRPO RL tuning, with UIUC) shows the team is pushing the research frontier rather than productizing.
one_paragraph_differentiator: OpenManus is the open-source general computer-use agent — the third leg of the 2025 computer-use tripod alongside browser-use (browser-only, DOM-centric) and Skyvern (browser-only, vision-first, enterprise). Where browser-use and Skyvern restrict the action space to a single browser tab, OpenManus exposes a polyglot toolbox (browser + terminal + filesystem + Python sandbox + GUI clicks) and is therefore the closest OSS analog to OpenAI Computer Use and Anthropic Computer Use. Unlike MetaGPT (its sibling project from the same authors), it is not a role-based multi-agent SDLC framework — it is a single-agent ReAct/ToolCall loop with an optional planner, deliberately simple. Its differentiator vs. OpenAI Agents SDK and LangChain is scope: it ships a working general-purpose agent out of the box rather than primitives.
known_anti_patterns: Viral hype risk — built in days as a response to closed-source Manus.im, so much of the early star count reflects narrative rather than capability. No published benchmarks in README. Multi-agent flow self-labeled 'unstable'. Memory model is thin. Issue/PR backlog suggests maintainer load. Watch for: rebrand/abandonment risk if MetaGPT team refocuses on OpenManus-RL research arm only.
